task_completion.description: Create a plane and position it above the table.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A plane object is visible in the 3D viewport, positioned noticeably above the table (Z-axis).

task_completion.description: Subdivide the plane to at least 10x10.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In Edit Mode or wireframe view, the plane shows a dense grid of subdivisions (at least 10x10 faces/100 vertices).

task_completion.description: Apply Collision physics to the TableTop and Ground objects.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Physics Properties tab shows 'Collision' enabled when the table or ground object is selected, or modifiers list shows a Collision modifier.

task_completion.description: Apply Cloth physics to the subdivided plane.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Physics Properties tab shows 'Cloth' enabled when the plane object is selected, or the modifiers list shows a Cloth modifier.

task_completion.description: Run the simulation so the cloth drapes naturally over the table.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The timeline playhead is advanced (around frame 50-60), and the plane in the 3D viewport is deformed, draping naturally over the edges of the table with visible folds.

task_completion.description: Save the project and export a render of the settled cloth.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Blender interface shows the file saved as 'cloth_drape.blend', and a rendered image window or file explorer shows 'cloth_render.png' in the /home/ga/BlenderProjects/ directory.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the Blender application.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the actual Blender GUI (viewport, outliner, properties panels) or Blender Python console being used, rather than just a text editor mocking files.

integrity.description: The cloth drape is the result of a physics simulation.
integrity.visual_evidence: The cloth shows natural, gravity-induced folds and wrinkles over the table's collision bounds, rather than manual vertex pulling or a flat, intersecting plane.

integrity.description: The rendered image matches the simulated scene.
integrity.visual_evidence: The final exported 'cloth_render.png' visually matches the draped cloth and table setup seen in the 3D viewport.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Verified facts: (1) Expected blend file: /home/ga/BlenderProjects/cloth_drape.blend. (2) Expected render image: /home/ga/BlenderProjects/cloth_render.png. (3) The cloth mesh must have at least 100 vertices (10x10 subdivisions). (4) The cloth object requires a CLOTH modifier, and the TableTop and Ground objects require COLLISION modifiers. (5) The cloth must show Z-variance deformation (threshold 0.005) indicating it has settled and draped over the table.
